title: Forager's Fortune
text: A patch of wild herbs clings to the roots of an ancient oak, half-hidden under fallen leaves. Someone gathered here before—there's a worn wicker basket nearby, left behind or forgotten.

choices.text: Pick what you can carry
Harvest everything and take the basket
Leave it. Someone may come back for this.

title: A Shady Hollow
text: Tucked between two ancient oaks, a natural hollow forms a near-perfect shelter—soft ground, a windbreak of dense brush, clear sightlines in every direction. Your feet ache. The light is still good.

choices.text: Make camp here while conditions favor it
Note the location and press on

title: Bubbling Spring
text: A small spring bubbles up between mossy stones at the edge of the path. The water runs clear and cold. You could smell it before you could see it.

choices.text: Drink deeply and rest your legs a while
Fill waterskins and keep moving
Walk past. No time.

title: Abandoned Campsite
text: A camp abandoned in haste—embers still faintly warm, a bedroll left behind, a half-eaten meal going cold. Whoever was here, they didn't take everything. Didn't have time to.

choices.text: Search the camp thoroughly
Look for signs of what happened here
Don't touch anything. Someone may be coming back.

title: Wildflower Meadow
text: A break in the canopy reveals a meadow bursting with color. Bees move lazily through tall grass. It is, for a moment, absurdly peaceful. The kind of place people paint pictures of.

choices.text: Stop and breathe. Just for a moment.
Gather a handful—someone in the next town might want them
Beautiful. Keep moving.

title: Old Milestone Stone
text: A weathered milestone stone sits at a crossroads, tilted with age. The original engravings are mostly faded, but you can make out a direction: "Three Days East—Redfield." Below that, in fresher, rougher scratchings: "Don't."

choices.text: Study the stone carefully
Make a mental note and continue
Keep walking

title: Soft Rain
text: A light rain begins to fall, pattering against the canopy overhead and dripping down in fat drops. The path ahead softens to mud. The forest smells of earth and wet bark.

choices.text: Find shelter and wait it out
Push through—a little rain never killed anyone
Walk slowly. Enjoy it.

title: The Birch Twins
text: Two ancient birch trees have grown together over decades, their trunks intertwined into a single massive double-spired form. Their roots cradle a space of soft, dry earth. Locals probably had a name for this place once.

choices.text: Rest in their roots tonight
Add your name to the bark
Admire it for a moment and move on

title: Mushroom Ring
text: A perfect ring of large, spotted mushrooms grows around the base of a dead elm. They look edible. They also look exactly like the ones your herbalist once warned you about. Hard to say which.

choices.text: Harvest carefully—you're fairly confident they're safe
Try one. If you're still standing in ten minutes, harvest the rest.
Not worth the risk
